back_translation: And the Ziphites came to Saul at Gibeah, saying, 'Is not David hiding on the hill of Hachilah, which is opposite the Jeshimon?'
And Saul arose and went down to the wilderness of Ziph, and with him three thousand chosen men of Israel, to seek David in the wilderness of Ziph.
And Saul encamped on the hill of Hachilah, which is opposite the Jeshimon, by the road; and David was staying in the wilderness, and he saw that Saul had come after him into the wilderness.
And David sent spies, and he knew that Saul had come with certainty.

And David arose and came to the place where Saul had encamped, and David saw the place where Saul lay, and Abner son of Ner, the captain of his army; and Saul was lying within the inner circle of the camp, and the people were camped around him.

And David answered and said to Ahimelech the Hittite, and to Abishai son of Zeruiah, brother of Joab, saying, 'Who will go down with me to Saul to the camp?' And Abishai said, 'I will go down with you.'

And David and Abishai came to the people at night, and behold, Saul was lying asleep within the inner circle, and his spear was thrust into the ground at his head; and Abner and the people were lying around him.
And Abishai said to David, 'Today God has shut up your enemy into your hand. And now please let me strike him with the spear, into the ground, one stroke; and I will not strike him a second time.'
And David said to Abishai, 'Do not destroy him, for who has stretched out his hand against the LORD's anointed and been guiltless?'
And David said, 'As the LORD lives — for either the LORD will strike him, or his day will come and he will die, or in battle he will go down and be swept away.'
Far be it from me by the LORD, from stretching out my hand against the LORD's anointed. And now please take the spear that is at his head, and the jug of water, and let us go for ourselves.'

And David took the spear and the jug of water from the head of Saul, and they went for themselves, and there was no one seeing, and no one knowing, and no one waking, for all of them were sleeping, because a deep sleep from the LORD had fallen upon them.
And David crossed to the other side and stood on the top of the mountain from afar; great was the place between them.
And David called out to the people and to Abner son of Ner, saying, 'Will you not answer, Abner?' And Abner answered and said, 'Who are you, who calls to the king?'
And David said to Abner, 'Are you not a man? And who is like you in Israel? And why have you not guarded over your lord the king? For one of the people came to destroy the king your lord.

Not good is this thing that you have done. As the LORD lives, you are sons of death, because you have not guarded over your lord, over the LORD's anointed. And now look — where is the king's spear, and the jug of water that was at his head?'
And Saul recognized David's voice, and said, 'Is this your voice, my son David?' And David said, 'My voice, my lord the king.'
And he said, 'Why is this — my lord pursues after his servant? For what have I done, and what is in my hand of evil?

And now, please let my lord the king hear the words of his servant: If the LORD has stirred you up against me, may He smell an offering; but if sons of man, cursed are they before the LORD, for they have driven me out today from sharing in the inheritance of the LORD, saying, Go, serve other gods.
And now, let not my blood fall to the ground from before the face of the LORD, for the king of Israel has come out to seek one flea, as one would pursue a partridge in the mountains.'

And Saul said, 'I have sinned. Return, my son David, for I will not do you harm again, in place of that my life was precious in your eyes this day. Behold, I have played the fool and have erred exceedingly.'
And David answered and said, 'Behold, the king's spear. And let one of the young men cross over and take it.
And the LORD will repay to the man his righteousness and his faithfulness, who the LORD gave you today into hand, and I would not consent to stretch out my hand against the LORD's anointed.
And behold, as your life was great in my eyes this day, so let my life be great in the eyes of the LORD, and let Him deliver me from all distress.'
And Saul said to David, 'Blessed are you, my son David — both doing you shall do, and prevailing you shall prevail.' And David went on his way, and Saul returned to his place.
